Title: User can log in successfully
Preconditions: User has a valid username and password
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter the username "admin" in the username field.
3. Enter the password "password" in the password field.
4. Click the "Login" button.
Expected Result: The user is logged in successfully and redirected to the main page.

Title: User cannot log in with invalid credentials
Preconditions: User enters incorrect username or password
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the login page.
2. Enter the username "invalid" in the username field.
3. Enter the password "password" in the password field.
4. Click the "Login" button.
Expected Result: An error message is displayed, and the user is not logged in.

Title: User can view their profile
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the user profile page.
2. Verify that the user's name and email are displayed.
Expected Result: The user's name and email are displayed on the profile page.

Title: User cannot view another user's profile
Preconditions: User is logged in as a regular user, not an admin
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to another user's profile page.
Expected Result: An error message is displayed, and the user is not able to view the other user's profile.

Title: User can view the noticeboard
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the noticeboard page.
2. Verify that the latest notice is displayed at the top of the page.
Expected Result: The latest notice is displayed at the top of the noticeboard page.

Title: User can create a new notice
Preconditions: User is logged in as an admin
Test Data: Title: Test Notice, Content: This is a test notice.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the noticeboard page.
2. Click the "Create Notice" button.
3. Enter the title "Test Notice" in the title field.
4. Enter the content "This is a test notice." in the content field.
5. Click the "Create" button.
Expected Result: The new notice is created and displayed on the noticeboard page.

Title: User can edit an existing notice
Preconditions: User is logged in as an admin, and there is at least one notice on the noticeboard
Test Data: Title: Updated Test Notice, Content: This is an updated test notice.
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the noticeboard page.
2. Find the notice to be edited.
3. Click the "Edit" button next to the notice.
4. Enter the title "Updated Test Notice" in the title field.
5. Enter the content "This is an updated test notice." in the content field.
6. Click the "Update" button.
Expected Result: The edited notice is displayed on the noticeboard page.

Title: User can delete a notice
Preconditions: User is logged in as an admin, and there is at least one notice on the noticeboard
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the noticeboard page.
2. Find the notice to be deleted.
3. Click the "Delete" button next to the notice.
4. Confirm the deletion in the popup.
Expected Result: The notice is removed from the noticeboard page.

Title: User cannot delete a notice they did not create
Preconditions: User is logged in as an admin, and there is at least one notice on the noticeboard
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the noticeboard page.
2. Find a notice created by another user.
3. Click the "Delete" button next to the notice.
Expected Result: An error message is displayed, and the notice is not deleted.

Title: User can view notice details
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the noticeboard page.
2. Find a notice and click on it.
3. Verify that the notice details (title, content, and creation date) are displayed.
Expected Result: The notice details are displayed on the notice details page.

Title: User cannot view notice details for a notice they did not create
Preconditions: User is logged in as a regular user, and there is at least one notice on the noticeboard
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the noticeboard page.
2. Find a notice created by another user and click on it.
Expected Result: An error message is displayed, and the user is not able to view the notice details.

Title: User receives notifications for new announcements and notices
Preconditions: User is logged in
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the notifications page.
2. Verify that there are no notifications displayed.
3. Create a new announcement or notice.
4. Refresh the notifications page.
5. Verify that there is at least one notification displayed.
Expected Result: The user receives a notification for the new announcement or notice.

Title: User can mark notifications as read
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one unread notification
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the notifications page.
2. Verify that there is at least one unread notification displayed.
3. Click the "Mark as read" button next to the notification.
4. Refresh the notifications page.
5. Verify that the notification is no longer displayed.
Expected Result: The user can mark notifications as read.

Title: User cannot mark a notification as read if it has already been read
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one read notification
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the notifications page.
2. Verify that there is at least one read notification displayed.
3. Click the "Mark as read" button next to the notification.
Expected Result: An error message is displayed, and the notification remains marked as read.

Title: User can view notification details
Preconditions: User is logged in and has at least one notification
Test Steps:
1. Navigate to the notifications page.
2. Find a notification and click on it.
3. Verify that the notification details (title, content, and creation date) are displayed.
Expected Result: The notification details are displayed on the notification details page.

Title: User cannot view notification details for a notification they did not create

Test Steps:

2. Find a notification created by another user and click on it.
Expected Result: An error message is displayed, and the user is not able to view the notification details.
